message:
feat(ui): consistent dot-separator format for detail metadata and Alt+? help toggle

Two improvements to TUI consistency and ergonomics:

1. Detail pane metadata format: Changed the second metadata line from
   bracket-wrapped format [key:value] to dot-separated format key:value
   with " · " separators between chips. This matches the footer HUD and
   results overview bar styling that was updated in 994e4b35, making the
   entire TUI use a single consistent format for key-value metadata.

   Before: [at:2026-01-31 20:31] [dur:1m 33s] [msgs:78 msgs]
   After:  at:2026-01-31 20:31 · dur:1m 33s · msgs:78 msgs

   The push_chip closure now tracks chip_count to insert " · " between
   items instead of wrapping each in brackets, and width accounting uses
   +1 for the colon (instead of +4 for brackets) plus conditional +3
   for the separator on non-first items.

2. Help toggle keybinding: Changed Alt+H → Alt+? to avoid conflict
   with vim-style Alt+h directional navigation. Updated the help overlay
   title, help content text, and corresponding test.

Snapshot files updated to reflect the new metadata format in the detail
pane header across all detail-related baseline snapshots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
